title: Roku Q4 2022 Earnings Report
text: Roku exceeded its own projections, reporting $867.1 million in net revenue for Q4 2022. The company had previously cautioned investors to expect around $800 million, with analysts predicting $804.19 million. Despite this positive surprise, the company's Q1 2023 outlook remains conservative at $700 million due to macroeconomic concerns. The streaming platform reached 70 million active accounts globally in 2022, up from 65.4 million in Q3. Roku also recorded a 19% year-over-year increase in streaming hours, totaling 87.4 billion globally for the year and 23.9 billion in Q4. Operating losses widened significantly to $249.9 million compared to $147 million in the previous quarter. In response, Roku implemented a cost-reduction strategy, cutting 200 U.S. jobs between Q4 2022 and Q1 2023. The company announced its first Roku-branded televisions launching spring 2023 and closed content deals with Warner Bros. Discovery (2,000 hours of programming) and Pocket.watch (exclusive children's content). Additionally, Roku's operating system captured 38% of U.S. smart TV unit sales in Q4 2022.
